summary_one_line: Input must contain a well-formed HTTP/ response header block so at least one header gets stored, making the cleanup loop iterate (true side).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes that pass the HTTP header parser so that a Curl_header_store is appended to data->state.httphdrs (a non-empty list head). This requires the literal token 'HTTP/' near offset 0x24 followed by parseable name:value header lines (e.g. 'Set-Cookie', 'Connect...', CRLF terminators 0d 0a). Only then is httphdrs.head non-NULL and the for-loop true side taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'HTTP/' token at offsets 0x24-0x29 (47 48 54 54 50 2f = 'GHTTP/' / 'HHTTP/') plus header-line structure with CRLF (0d 0a) and header names ('Set-Coo', 'TranEfe', 'Connect'); they reach Curl_headers_push 36 times (W hits) so headers are actually stored and the cleanup loop iterates 3000+ times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (32-36 bytes) and carry no 'HTTP/' token at 0x24 (instead 25/2b/2e bytes — '%','+','.' filler); Curl_headers_push has 0 L hits so no header is ever stored, httphdrs.head stays NULL, and the loop false side is taken (blocked for the full 24h).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact comparison operand ('HTTP/' and header-token byte sequences) into the input at matching offsets, synthesizing the well-formed header bytes that naive/value_profile only reach by blind havoc. The lineage of a winning cmplog seed contains ParentInfo-only dash rows, the I2S floor signal under the current build.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the cleanup loop condition iterating over the stored-headers list head.
Both decisive pairs are I2S-delta pairs with full prob_div=1.00.
Winner-resolving seeds contain the literal HTTP/ token bytes.
Winner seeds carry header-line structure with CRLF and a Set-Cookie header name.
Headers are actually stored only in the winner: Curl_headers_push runs 36 times for W and 0 for L.
The loop true side is taken only in the winner: L takes zero true iterations at the blocker line.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 348  T=26 F=60  T=0 F=60  for(e = data->state.httphdrs.head; e; e = n) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds are short and lack the HTTP/ token, carrying filler bytes instead.
At offset 0x24 winner bytes concentrate on G/H while loser bytes are P/B/other.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one intercepted CMP operand at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, explaining why it loses to vpc on this I2S pair.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 78 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 00a044736f809ce4`; the 29-level ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 27 and 28, the lower-bound I2SRandReplace signal under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the loop on a stored HTTP header in which cmplog and naive resolve the true side at equal rates (no I2S advantage), i.e. the HTTP/ + header-token byte sequence is reachable by blind havoc as easily as by operand substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact set of byte sequences I2SRandReplace must substitute (HTTP/ plus which header names) is inferred from seed bytes and source; the CMP operands that gate header storage are not directly listed in the prompt.
